title: John Krakauer named director of Champalimaud's Centre for Restorative Neurotechnology
gist: SFI External Professor John Krakauer was named director of Champalimaud's Centre for Restorative Neurotechnology in Lisbon, a center focused on converging neuroscience, technology, and medicine to turn movement and stroke research into deployable neuro-rehabilitation therapies.

insights.claim: The Centre for Restorative Neurotechnology at the Champalimaud Foundation in Lisbon aims to bring together neuroscience, technology, and medicine to turn research into treatments for neurological and psychiatric conditions.
insights.speaker: Santa Fe Institute
insights.so_what: Signals institutional momentum toward commercializing neurotech therapies, a watch item for the neuro-rehabilitation and medical-device space.
insights.quote: aiming to bring together neuroscience, technology, and medicine to turn research into treatments for neurological and psychiatric conditions

insights.claim: Krakauer's work is aimed at treatment rather than just understanding, and he has co-founded efforts to turn studies of human movement into therapies including motion-controlled games used in stroke rehabilitation.
insights.speaker: Santa Fe Institute
insights.so_what: Points to a translational pipeline from movement neuroscience into digital-health and gamified rehab products.
insights.quote: motion-controlled games used in stroke rehabilitation

themes.theme: Intelligence research across biology and machines

insights.claim: Krakauer and SFI Resident Professor Melanie Mitchell co-organized a working group on the nature of intelligence, bringing together neuroscience, psychology, philosophy, and AI to examine intelligence in both biological systems and machines.
insights.speaker: Santa Fe Institute
insights.so_what: Indicates cross-disciplinary scrutiny of what 'intelligence' means, relevant to grounding claims about AI capability.
insights.locator: Krakauer first came to the Santa Fe Institute
